content_en:
One Incident That Highlights ‘Umar Ibn ‘Abdul~Azeez’s Fear Of Allah; And Another Chain From Thaubaan ate

Al’Abbaas ibn Saalim ibn Al-Lakhmee said, “Umar ibn ‘Abdul- ‘Azeez sent to Abu Salaam Al-Habashee, asking him about the

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 33%

Haud... He went to ‘Umar, who asked him. He said, “I heard Thaubaan say, ‘I heard the Messenger of Allah #@ say: Indeed my Haud is from ‘Adn to ‘Ammaan Al-Balgaa. lis water is whiter thar: milk and sweeter than honey. Its drinking glasses are equal in number to the stars. Whoever drinks a dose from it will never thereafter be thirsty. The first of people to come and drink from it are the poor Muhanjireen (those who migrated from Makkah to Madeenah).” ‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab a asked, “Who are they, O Messenger of Allah?” He #% said, “They are the ones with disheveled heads and dirty garments, those who do not marry AlMutana‘imaat Al-Mutamattai’aat (comfortable, soft, pleasurable, etc.), those for whom closed doors are not opened.” ‘Umar ibn ‘Abdul- ‘Azeez said, “I indeed married Al-Mutana'imaat and closed (doors) were opened for me...unless Allah has mercy on-me. By Allah, I will not apply oil to my hair until it becomes disheveled, and T will not ‘wash my garment, which has worn out my body, until it becomes dirty.” (Ahmad) At-Tirmidhee and Ibn Maajah related it as well, but through different chains of narrators.

‘The Narration of Jaabir Ibn Samarah : “The Messenger 3% Is The Faratu (Predecessor, LE., The First Before Them) Of His Nation To Come To The Hand On The Day Of Resurrection”

Jaabir ibn Samarah # related that the Messenger of Allah % said, “Indeed J am your Farat (predecessor, ic. the first) to the Haud (Basin). And the distance between its two sides is like what is

332 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

between San‘aa and Ailah, It is as if the jugs in it are the stars (in number).” (Related by Abu Ya'laa) Muslim related the same from Abu Hammaam, and Allah # knows best.

Another Narration From Jaabir Ibn Samarahes

‘Aamir ibn Sa’ad ibn Abee Waqqaas said, “I sent a letter with my servant, Naafai’, to Jaabir ibn Samarah 4s: ‘Inform me of something you heard from the Messenger of Allah #&.’ He wrote to me, saying, ‘Indeed I heard him 2 say, ‘I am the first to reach the Haud’” (Muslim)

The Narration Of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah ts

Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah & related that the Messenger of Allah 3% said, “T (will be) at the Haud, looking at those who come to me. People before me will be taken, and I will say, ‘O my Lord, these are from me and from my nation.’ It will be said, ‘And what makes you know what they did after you? After you, they continued to turn back on their heels (some scholars say that this is referring to those who apostatized during the beginning of Abu Bakr's caliphate).”” (Ahmad)

In another narration, Jaabir -& related that the Messenger of Allah #¢ said, “The Haud is the distance of a month, and its corners — meaning its width — is like its length. Its Keezaan (mugs) are like the stars of the sky (in number). (Its drink) is more fragrant than musk (best kind of perfume) and whiter than milk, Whoever drinks from it will never thereafter be thirsty.” (Ahmad) The chain of this narration is authentic and it fulfills the conditions of Muslim, though he didn’t relate it himself,

On The Day Of Resurrection, The Messenger 2 Will Want His Nation To Outnumber (Other Nations); He Ordered Them Not To Return As Disbelievers After Him By Killing One Another

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 333

between Ailah,” the narrator said, | think he said, “and Makkah. In it are Makaayil (perhaps a kind of mug or cup, and Allah knows best) that are more than the number of the stars. No sooner does a believer take one and then place it with his hand than his brother takes it.” (Related by Abu Bakr Al-Bazzaar.) And Ibn Abee AdDunyaa related it through a different chain.

The Narration Of Jundub Ibn ‘Abdullah Al-Bajalee

Jundub # said, “I heard the Prophet # say, “I am your Farat (predecessor, the one who will precede you) to the Haud (Basin).” (Bukhaaree) Muslim and Imam Ahmad related this narration, but through different chains. Sufyaan said, “Al-Farat is one who precedes.”

The Narration Of Jaariyah Ibn Wahb Al-Khuzaa’ee 4

The Narration Of Hudhaifah Ibn Usaid &

Hudhaifah ibn Usaid .& said, “When the Prophet #4 came back from. the Farewell Pilgrimage, he #% said, ‘O people, indeed I am your Farat (predecessor, the one to precede you) to the Haud. Indeed you will come and drink from a Haud, which in width, is as the distance between Basrah and San’aa. In it are cups which axe equal in number to the stars.” (Related by Abu Shuraihah Al-Ghaffaaree) No author of any compilation related it, nor did Ahmad relate it.

‘The Narration Of Hudhaifah Ibn Al-Yamaan Al-‘Absee -t

Hudhaifah ibn Al-Yamaan ¥% related that the Messenger of Allah 38 said, “My Haud is further than Ailah and ‘Adan (.e. it is longer than the distance from Al-Madeenah to Ailah or the distance between AlMadeenah and ‘Adan; and Allah #% knows best). And by the One Who has my soul in His Hand, its cups outnumber the stars. (Its

334 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

drink) is whiter than milk and sweeter than honey. And by the One Who has my soul in His hand, I will drive men away from it just asa man drives away stranger camels from his basin.” It was said, “O Messenger of Allah, you will know (or recognize) us at that time?” He 3 said, “Yes, you will come to me, Ghurr (whiteness in the forehead; the faces of the believers will shine on the Day of Resurrection), Muhajjaloon (the places they wash in their Wudoo, ablution, will shine) from the effects of Al-Wudoo (the ablution). And it is not for anyone other than you.” (Related by Abul-Qaasim AlBaghawee. Muslim related it through another chain from Hudhaifah from the Prophet #%, and Allah 4% knows best.

The Narration Of Zaid bn Argam .ts

Zaid ibn Arqam 4 said, “We were with the Messenger of Allah 2% when he camped somewhere, and I heard him say, “You do not constitute one part of 100,000 parts from those of my nation who will come and drink from the Haud.’” Abu Hamzah asked Zaid ote, “How many were you at that time?” He & said, “700 or 800.” (Ahmad) Abu Daawood related it through a different chain of narrators, The Abu Hamzah mentioned above is Talhah ibn Yazeed Al-Ansaaree, the freed slave of Qurdhah Ibn Ka’ab. And Allah 3% knows best.

The Hellfire Is The Reward Of Those Who Intentionally Lie Upon The Messenger Of Allah 2.

“Ubaidullah ibn Ziyaad said to Zaid ibn Argam, “What about the Ahtwadeeth that have reached me from you, Ahaadeeth that you relate from the Messenger of Allah? You claim that he has a Haud (Basin) in Paradise?” Zaid .& said, “The Messenger of Allah #@ said that to us and promised it to us.” ‘Ubaidullah ibn Ziyaad said, “You have lied, or rather you are an old man who has become a dotard.” Zaid #e said, “Lo! Indeed my two ears heard it from the Messenger of Allah #g, I heard him say, ‘Whoever lies upon me intentionally, then let him take his seat in the Hellfire’ And I have not lied upon the Messenger of Allah 2.” (Al-Baihagee)

Regarding the Haud, Ibn Khuzaimah (may Allah have mercy on him) related a hadith from Salmaan Al-Faarisee &, who said, “The Messenger of Allah #8 delivered a sermon to us on the last day of Sha’baan. He 9 said, ‘O people, a great and blessed month has

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 335

given you shade.” The hadith continues, with a mention of the virtues of Ramadan, until he # said, “Whoever fills a fasting person during it, then Allah will give him a dose to drink from my Haud @asin), after which he will not again be thirsty until he enters Paradise.”

On The Day Of Resurrection, Every Prophet Has A Haud (Basin); They Will Be Vying With One Another - Which Among Them Has The Most People Coming To His Basin To Drink From It

The Narration Of Samarah Ibn Jundub Al-Fizaaree &

Samarah ibn Jundub # related that the Prophet 2% said, “Every Prophet has a Haud (Basin); they will be vying with one another — which among them has the most people come to his basin to drink from it. And I indeed hope to have more people coming to drink (from my Basin) than each one of them (has coming to his).”” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee ‘Aasim) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain and then said, “This hadith is Ghareeb.” And Allah # knows best.
content_id:
Satu Kisah yang Menyoroti Rasa Takut 'Umar ibn 'Abdul-'Azeez kepada Allah; dan Sanad Lain dari Thaubaan Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Al-'Abbaas ibn Saalim ibn Al-Lakhmee berkata, "'Umar ibn 'Abdul-'Azeez mengirim (surat) kepada Abu Salaam Al-Habasyee, menanyakan kepadanya tentang

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar 331

Haud... Ia pergi kepada 'Umar, yang menanyainya. Ia berkata, 'Aku mendengar Thauban berkata, "Aku mendengar Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: Sesungguhnya Haud-ku membentang dari 'Adn sampai 'Ammaan Al-Balqa. Airnya lebih putih dari susu dan lebih manis dari madu. Gelas-gelas minumnya sama jumlahnya dengan bintang-bintang. Barangsiapa minum satu teguk darinya tidak akan haus lagi setelahnya. Yang pertama dari manusia datang dan minum darinya adalah orang-orang Muhajirin yang miskin (mereka yang berhijrah dari Makkah ke Madinah)."' 'Umar ibn Al-Khattaab radhiyallahu 'anhu bertanya, 'Siapa mereka, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, 'Mereka adalah orang-orang dengan rambut kusut dan pakaian kotor, yang tidak menikah dengan Al-Mutana'imaat Al-Mutamattai'aat (wanita yang nyaman, lembut, mewah, dsb.), mereka yang pintu-pintu tertutup tidak dibuka untuknya.' 'Umar ibn 'Abdul-'Azeez berkata, 'Sungguh aku telah menikah dengan Al-Mutana'imaat dan (pintu-pintu) tertutup telah dibukakan untukku...kecuali jika Allah merahmati aku. Demi Allah, aku tidak akan mengolesi rambut ku dengan minyak hingga ia menjadi kusut, dan aku tidak akan mencuci pakaianku, yang telah menyiksa tubuhku, hingga ia menjadi kotor.'" (Ahmad) At-Tirmidzi dan Ibn Maajah juga meriwayatkannya, tetapi melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Riwayat Jaabir Ibn Samarah Radhiyallahu 'Anhu: "Rasulullah Shallallahu 'Alaihi Wa Sallam Adalah Farat (Pendahulu) Umatnya untuk Tiba di Haud pada Hari Kiamat"

Jaabir ibn Samarah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Sesungguhnya aku adalah Farat (pendahulu, yakni yang pertama) kalian menuju Haud (Telaga). Dan jarak antara dua sisinya adalah seperti

332 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

antara San'aa dan Ailah. Seolah-olah kendi-kendi di dalamnya seperti bintang-bintang (jumlahnya)." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Ya'laa) Muslim meriwayatkan hal yang sama dari Abu Hammaam, dan Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala lebih mengetahui.

Riwayat Lain dari Jaabir Ibn Samarah Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

'Aamir ibn Sa'ad ibn Abee Waqqaas berkata, "Aku mengutus suratku dengan pelayanku, Naafi', kepada Jaabir ibn Samarah radhiyallahu 'anhu: 'Beritahukan kepadaku sesuatu yang kamu dengar dari Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam.' Ia menulis kepadaku, 'Sesungguhnya aku mendengar beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Aku adalah yang pertama tiba di Haud."'" (Muslim)

Riwayat Jaabir Ibn 'Abdullah Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Jaabir ibn 'Abdullah radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Aku (akan berada) di Haud, memandang orang-orang yang datang kepadaku. Orang-orang akan diambil sebelumku, dan aku akan berkata, 'Wahai Tuhanku, mereka adalah dari aku dan dari umatku.' Akan dikatakan, 'Dan apa yang membuatmu tahu apa yang mereka lakukan setelahmu? Setelahmu, mereka terus berbalik ke belakang (sebagian ulama mengatakan bahwa ini merujuk kepada mereka yang murtad pada awal kekhalifahan Abu Bakr).'" (Ahmad)

Dalam riwayat lain, Jaabir radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Haud itu berjarak sebulan perjalanan, dan sudut-sudutnya — maksudnya lebarnya — seperti panjangnya. Keezaan-nya (cangkir-cangkirnya) seperti bintang-bintang langit (jumlahnya). (Minumannya) lebih harum dari kesturi (jenis parfum terbaik) dan lebih putih dari susu. Barangsiapa minum darinya tidak akan haus lagi setelahnya." (Ahmad) Sanad riwayat ini sahih dan memenuhi syarat-syarat Muslim, meskipun beliau tidak meriwayatkannya sendiri.

Pada Hari Kiamat, Rasulullah Shallallahu 'Alaihi Wa Sallam Menginginkan Umatnya Melebihi (Jumlah Umat Lain); Beliau Memerintahkan Mereka Agar Tidak Kembali Menjadi Kafir Setelahnya dengan Saling Membunuh

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar 333

antara Ailah," perawi berkata, aku pikir ia berkata, "dan Makkah. Di dalamnya terdapat Makaayil (mungkin sejenis cangkir atau gelas, dan Allah lebih mengetahui) yang jumlahnya melebihi bintang-bintang. Tidak lebih cepat seorang mukmin mengambil satu dan meletakkannya dengan tangannya daripada saudaranya mengambilnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr Al-Bazzaar.) Dan Ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkannya melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Riwayat Jundub Ibn 'Abdullah Al-Bajalee Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Jundub radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Aku mendengar Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, 'Aku adalah Farat (pendahulu, orang yang akan mendahuluimu) kalian menuju Haud (Telaga).'" (Bukhari) Muslim dan Imam Ahmad meriwayatkan riwayat ini, tetapi melalui sanad yang berbeda. Sufyaan berkata, "Al-Farat adalah orang yang mendahului."

Riwayat Jaariyah Ibn Wahb Al-Khuzaa'ee Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Riwayat Hudhaifah Ibn Usaid Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Hudhaifah ibn Usaid radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Ketika Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam kembali dari Haji Wada', beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, 'Wahai manusia, sesungguhnya aku adalah Farat (pendahulu, orang yang mendahuluimu) kalian menuju Haud. Sesungguhnya kalian akan datang dan minum dari Haud, yang lebarnya adalah seperti jarak antara Basrah dan San'aa. Di dalamnya terdapat cangkir-cangkir yang jumlahnya sama dengan bintang-bintang.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Shuraihah Al-Ghaffaaree) Tidak ada penulis kompilasi manapun yang meriwayatkannya, dan Ahmad pun tidak meriwayatkannya.

Riwayat Hudhaifah Ibn Al-Yamaan Al-'Absee Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Hudhaifah ibn Al-Yamaan radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Haud-ku lebih jauh dari Ailah dan 'Adan (yakni lebih panjang dari jarak Al-Madeenah ke Ailah atau jarak antara Al-Madeenah dan 'Adan; dan Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala lebih mengetahui). Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, cangkir-cangkirnya melebihi jumlah bintang-bintang. (Minumannya

334 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar

lebih putih dari susu dan lebih manis dari madu. Demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, sungguh aku akan mengusir orang-orang darinya sebagaimana seorang laki-laki mengusir unta-unta asing dari telaga miliknya." Dikatakan, "Wahai Rasulullah, kamu akan mengenal (atau mengenali) kami pada saat itu?" Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Ya, kalian akan datang kepadaku, Ghurr (keputihan di dahi; wajah-wajah orang beriman akan bersinar pada Hari Kiamat), Muhajjaloon (bagian-bagian yang mereka basuh dalam Wudhu mereka akan bersinar) dari bekas Al-Wudhu (bersuci). Dan itu bukan untuk siapapun selain kalian." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abul-Qaasim Al-Baghawee). Muslim meriwayatkannya melalui sanad lain dari Hudhaifah dari Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala lebih mengetahui.

Riwayat Zaid ibn Arqam Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Zaid ibn Arqam radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Kami bersama Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam ketika beliau singgah di suatu tempat, dan aku mendengar beliau bersabda, 'Kalian tidak membentuk satu bagian dari 100.000 bagian dari anggota umatku yang akan datang dan minum dari Haud.'" Abu Hamzah bertanya kepada Zaid radhiyallahu 'anhu, "Berapa banyak kalian saat itu?" Ia radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "700 atau 800." (Ahmad) Abu Dawud meriwayatkannya melalui sanad yang berbeda. Abu Hamzah yang disebutkan di atas adalah Talhah ibn Yazeed Al-Ansaaree, budak yang dimerdekakan dari Qurdhah ibn Ka'ab. Dan Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala lebih mengetahui.

Neraka Adalah Balasan bagi Mereka yang Sengaja Berbohong atas Nama Rasulullah Shallallahu 'Alaihi Wa Sallam

'Ubaidullah ibn Ziyaad berkata kepada Zaid ibn Arqam, "Bagaimana dengan hadith-hadith yang sampai kepadaku darimu, hadith-hadith yang kamu riwayatkan dari Rasulullah? Kamu mengklaim bahwa beliau memiliki Haud (Telaga) di Surga?" Zaid radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam mengatakan itu kepada kami dan menjanjikannya kepada kami." 'Ubaidullah ibn Ziyaad berkata, "Kamu berbohong, atau lebih tepatnya kamu adalah orang tua yang sudah pikun." Zaid radhiyallahu 'anhu berkata, "Ketahuilah! Sesungguhnya kedua telingaku mendengarnya dari Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam. Aku mendengar beliau bersabda, 'Barangsiapa berbohong atas namaku dengan sengaja, maka hendaklah ia mengambil tempat duduknya di Neraka.' Dan aku tidak pernah berbohong atas nama Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam." (Al-Baihagee)

Mengenai Haud, Ibn Khuzaimah (semoga Allah merahmatinya) meriwayatkan sebuah hadith dari Salmaan Al-Faarisee radhiyallahu 'anhu, yang berkata, "Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam berkhutbah kepada kami pada hari terakhir Sya'ban. Beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, 'Wahai manusia, sebuah bulan yang agung dan penuh berkah telah

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Malapetaka Besar 335

menaungi kalian.'" Hadith itu berlanjut, dengan menyebut keutamaan-keutamaan Ramadan, hingga beliau shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Barangsiapa mengenyangkan orang yang berpuasa pada bulan itu, maka Allah akan memberinya satu teguk minum dari Haud-ku (Telaga-ku), setelah itu ia tidak akan haus lagi hingga ia masuk Surga."

Pada Hari Kiamat, Setiap Nabi Memiliki Haud (Telaga); Mereka Akan Berlomba-lomba — Siapa di Antara Mereka yang Paling Banyak Orang Datang ke Telaganya untuk Minum Darinya

Riwayat Samarah Ibn Jundub Al-Fizaaree Radhiyallahu 'Anhu

Samarah ibn Jundub radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda, "Setiap Nabi memiliki Haud (Telaga); mereka akan berlomba-lomba — siapa di antara mereka yang paling banyak orang datang ke telaganya untuk minum darinya. Dan sungguh aku berharap memiliki lebih banyak orang yang datang minum (dari Telaga-ku) daripada masing-masing mereka (yang datang ke telaganya)." (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee 'Aasim) At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkannya melalui sanad yang berbeda kemudian berkata, "Hadith ini Ghareeb." Dan Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala lebih mengetahui.
content_ar: One Incident That Highlights ‘عمر Ibn ‘Abdul~Azeez’s الخوف Of الله; And Another Chain From Thaubaan ate Al’Abbaas ibn Saalim ibn Al-Lakhmee said, “عمر ibn ‘Abdul- ‘Azeez sent to Abu Salaam Al-Habashee, asking him about the الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 33% Haud... He went to ‘عمر, who asked him. قال, “I heard Thaubaan say, ‘I heard الرسول ﷺ of الله #@ say: Indeed my Haud is from ‘Adn to ‘Ammaan Al-Balgaa. lis water is whiter thar: milk and sweeter than honey. Its drinking glasses are equal in number to the stars. Whoever drinks a dose from it will never thereafter be thirsty. The أولاً of people to come and drink from it are the poor Muhanjireen (those who migrated from مكة المكرمة to Madeenah).” ‘عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab a asked, “Who are they, O Messenger of الله?” He #% said, “They are the ones with disheveled heads and dirty garments, those who do not marry AlMutana‘imaat Al-Mutamattai’aat (comfortable, soft, pleasurable, etc.), those for whom closed doors are not opened.” ‘عمر ibn ‘Abdul- ‘Azeez said, “I indeed married Al-Mutana'imaat and closed (doors) were opened for me...إلا إذا الله has الرحمة on-me. By الله, I will not apply oil to my hair حتى it becomes disheveled, and T will not ‘wash my garment, which has worn out my الجسد, حتى it becomes dirty.” (أحمد) At-Tirmidhee and Ibn Maajah related it as well, but through different chains of narrators. ‘The Narration of Jaabir Ibn Samarah : “الرسول ﷺ 3% Is The Faratu (Predecessor, LE., The أولاً قبل Them) Of His Nation To Come To The Hand On The Day Of البعث” Jaabir ibn Samarah # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله % said, “Indeed J am your Farat (predecessor, ic. the أولاً) to the Haud (Basin). And the distance between its two sides is like what is 332 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations between San‘aa and Ailah, It is as إذا the jugs in it are the stars (in number).” (Related by Abu Ya'laa) مسلم related the same from Abu Hammaam, and الله # knows best. Another Narration From Jaabir Ibn Samarahes ‘Aamir ibn Sa’ad ibn Abee Waqqaas said, “I sent a letter with my servant, Naafai’, to Jaabir ibn Samarah 4s: ‘Inform me of something you heard from الرسول ﷺ of الله #&.’ كتب to me, saying, ‘Indeed I heard him 2 say, ‘I am the أولاً to reach the Haud’” (مسلم) The Narration Of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah ts Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah & related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 3% said, “T (will be) at the Haud, looking at those who come to me. People قبل me will be taken, and I will say, ‘O my Lord, these are from me and from my nation.’ It will be said, ‘And what makes you know what they did بعد you? بعد you, they continued to turn back on their heels (some العلماء say that this is referring to those who apostatized خلال the beginning of أبو بكر's الخلافة).”” (أحمد) In another narration, Jaabir -& related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #¢ said, “The Haud is the distance of a month, and its corners — meaning its width — is like its length. Its Keezaan (mugs) are like the stars of the sky (in number). (Its drink) is more fragrant than musk (best kind of perfume) and whiter than milk, Whoever drinks from it will never thereafter be thirsty.” (أحمد) The chain of this narration is authentic and it fulfills the conditions of مسلم, though he didn’t relate it himself, On The Day Of البعث, الرسول ﷺ 2 Will Want His Nation To Outnumber (Other Nations); He Ordered Them Not To Return As Disbelievers بعد Him By Killing بعضهم بعضاً الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 333 between Ailah,” the narrator said, | think قال, “and مكة المكرمة. In it are Makaayil (perhaps a kind of mug or cup, and الله knows best) that are more than the number of the stars. No sooner does a المؤمن take one and then place it with his hand than his الأخ takes it.” (Related by أبو بكر Al-Bazzaar.) And Ibn Abee AdDunyaa related it through a different chain. The Narration Of Jundub Ibn ‘Abdullah Al-Bajalee Jundub # said, “I heard النبي ﷺ # say, “I am your Farat (predecessor, the one who will precede you) to the Haud (Basin).” (Bukhaaree) مسلم and الإمام أحمد related this narration, but through different chains. Sufyaan said, “Al-Farat is one who precedes.” The Narration Of Jaariyah Ibn Wahb Al-Khuzaa’ee 4 The Narration Of Hudhaifah Ibn Usaid & Hudhaifah ibn Usaid .& said, “عندما النبي ﷺ #4 came back from. the Farewell Pilgrimage, he #% said, ‘O people, indeed I am your Farat (predecessor, the one to precede you) to the Haud. Indeed you will come and drink from a Haud, which in width, is as the distance between Basrah and San’aa. In it are cups which axe equal in number to the stars.” (Related by Abu Shuraihah Al-Ghaffaaree) No author of any compilation related it, nor did أحمد relate it. ‘The Narration Of Hudhaifah Ibn Al-Yamaan Al-‘Absee -t Hudhaifah ibn Al-Yamaan ¥% related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 38 said, “My Haud is further than Ailah and ‘Adan (.e. it is longer than the distance from Al-Madeenah to Ailah or the distance between AlMadeenah and ‘Adan; and الله #% knows best). And by the One Who has my النفس in His Hand, its cups outnumber the stars. (Its 334 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations drink) is whiter than milk and sweeter than honey. And by the One Who has my النفس in His hand, I will drive men away from it just asa man drives away stranger camels from his basin.” It was said, “O Messenger of الله, you will know (or recognize) us at that time?” He 3 said, “Yes, you will come to me, Ghurr (whiteness in the forehead; the faces of the المؤمنون will shine on the Day of البعث), Muhajjaloon (the places they wash in their الوضوء, ablution, will shine) from the effects of Al-الوضوء (the ablution). And it is not for anyone other than you.” (Related by Abul-Qaasim AlBaghawee. مسلم related it through another chain from Hudhaifah from النبي ﷺ #%, and الله 4% knows best. The Narration Of Zaid bn Argam .ts Zaid ibn Arqam 4 said, “We were with الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% عندما he camped somewhere, and I heard him say, “You do not constitute one part of 100,000 parts from those of my nation who will come and drink from the Haud.’” Abu Hamzah asked Zaid ote, “How many were you at that time?” He & said, “700 or 800.” (أحمد) Abu Daawood related it through a different chain of narrators, The Abu Hamzah mentioned above is Talhah ibn Yazeed Al-Ansaaree, the freed slave of Qurdhah Ibn Ka’ab. And الله 3% knows best. The النار Is The الأجر Of Those Who Intentionally Lie Upon الرسول ﷺ Of الله 2. “Ubaidullah ibn Ziyaad said to Zaid ibn Argam, “What about the Ahtwadeeth that have reached me from you, Ahaadeeth that you relate from الرسول ﷺ of الله? You claim that he has a Haud (Basin) in الجنة?” Zaid .& said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله #@ said that to us and promised it to us.” ‘Ubaidullah ibn Ziyaad said, “You have lied, or rather you are an old man who has become a dotard.” Zaid #e said, “Lo! Indeed my two ears heard it from الرسول ﷺ of الله #g, I heard him say, ‘Whoever lies upon me intentionally, then let him take his seat in the النار’ And I have not lied upon الرسول ﷺ of الله 2.” (Al-Baihagee) فيما يخص the Haud, Ibn Khuzaimah (may الله have الرحمة on him) related a الحديث from Salmaan Al-Faarisee &, who said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 delivered a sermon to us on the last day of Sha’baan. He 9 said, ‘O people, a great and blessed month has الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 335 given you shade.” The الحديث continues, with a mention of the الفضائل of Ramadan, حتى he # said, “Whoever fills a الصيام person خلال it, then الله will give him a dose to drink from my Haud @asin), بعد which he will not again be thirsty حتى he enters الجنة.” On The Day Of البعث, Every النبي Has A Haud (Basin); They Will Be Vying With بعضهم بعضاً - Which Among Them Has The Most People Coming To His Basin To Drink From It The Narration Of Samarah Ibn Jundub Al-Fizaaree & Samarah ibn Jundub # related that النبي ﷺ 2% said, “Every النبي has a Haud (Basin); they will be vying with بعضهم بعضاً — which among them has the most people come to his basin to drink from it. And I indeed الأمل to have more people coming to drink (from my Basin) than each one of them (has coming to his).”” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abee ‘Aasim) At-Tirmidhee related it through a different chain and then said, “This الحديث is Ghareeb.” And الله # knows best.
